nodes.name: 婆罗洲
nodes.summary: 婆罗洲是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联91种鸟类，包括菲律宾冢雉、Bornean crestless fireback、红头林鹧鸪等91种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: 新幾內亞島
nodes.summary: 新幾內亞島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联312种鸟类，包括南方鶴鴕、鵲鵝、细斑树鸭等312种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: 中南半島
nodes.summary: 中南半島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联37种鸟类，包括白鹇、普通燕鸻、普通夜鹰等37种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 仙八色鸫
nodes.summary: 仙八色鸫（Fairy pitta，学名 Pitta nympha），在图谱中标记为易危（VU）物种，已关联7个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括猎捕压力、森林砍伐、栖息地破坏等4项。

nodes.name: 东南亚
nodes.summary: 东南亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联160种鸟类，包括棉鳧、琵嘴鸭、勺嘴鹬等160种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: VU
nodes.summary: VU表示 IUCN 易危等级，当前关联694种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、索马里鸵鸟、大斑几维鸟等694种鸟类。

nodes.name: 人类活动扰动
nodes.summary: 旅游、施工和高频活动影响繁殖或觅食。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 东亚
nodes.summary: 东亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联17种鸟类，包括花脸鸭、日本鵪鶉、大鸨等17种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 汶莱
nodes.summary: 汶莱是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联101种鸟类，包括婆羅洲地杜鵑、Short-toed coucal、Raffles's malkoha等101种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 加里曼丹
nodes.summary: 加里曼丹是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联6种鸟类，包括Moustached hawk-cuckoo、蘇门答腊角鸮、Scarlet-rumped trogon等6种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The fairy pitta breeds in East Asia and migrates south to winter in Southeast Asia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The fairy pitta breeds in East Asia and migrates south to winter in Southeast Asia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|right|Fairy pitta in southern Thailand The fairy pitta is a migratory species and travels from Northeast Asia, where it breeds in summer (April–September) to South and Southeast Asia to spend the winter (October–March). Sightings have been reported from India and Indochina, including both the Bruneian and Kalimantan sides of the island of Borneo, and New Guinea, as well as in China, North Korea, South Korea, and Taiwan

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|right|Fairy pitta in southern Thailand The fairy pitta is a migratory species and travels from Northeast Asia, where it breeds in summer (April–September) to South and Southeast Asia to spend the winter (October–March). Sightings have been reported from India and Indochina, including both the Bruneian and Kalimantan sides of the island of Borneo, and New Guinea, as well as in China, North Korea, South Korea, and Taiwan

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|right|Fairy pitta in southern Thailand The fairy pitta is a migratory species and travels from Northeast Asia, where it breeds in summer (April–September) to South and Southeast Asia to spend the winter (October–March). Sightings have been reported from India and Indochina, including both the Bruneian and Kalimantan sides of the island of Borneo, and New Guinea, as well as in China, North Korea, South Korea, and Taiwan

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Due to various habitat and anthropogenic disruptions, such as deforestation, wildfire, hunting, trapping, and cage-bird trade, the fairy pitta is rare and the population is declining in most places. Listed on the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora (CITES) Appendix II, this bird is classified as vulnerable on the IUCN Red List of Threatened Species

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Due to various habitat and anthropogenic disruptions, such as deforestation, wildfire, hunting, trapping, and cage-bird trade, the fairy pitta is rare and the population is declining in most places. Listed on the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species of Wild Fauna and Flora (CITES) Appendix II, this bird is classified as vulnerable on the IUCN Red List of Threatened Species

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The main causes for the decline are habitat loss, trapping, hunting, and human disturbances. The causes of habitat disruption, which encompasses deforestation in the bird's breeding range, are essentially the results of human activity, but the characteristics of threats vary from one region to another

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: In Yunlin County, Taiwan, the largest known breeding group of the species is being severely impacted by the flooding from the Hushan Dam project. Despite the imminent threats that the fairy pitta is facing in Taiwan, however, quantitative data on the impact of human disturbance on the population remains limited, undermining the conservation efforts. The threat of hunting has been prevalent in Guangxi, China and Taiwan
